_comment: RAT runtime/v1 conformance vectors — ADR-003 shared golden data. The SINGLE source of truth for the runtime axis. Both independent references (Go: plugins/runtime/inmemory-go, Python: plugins/runtime/inmemory-py) load THIS file and drive RuntimeService.Execute over real gRPC. runtime/v1 is the FIRST streaming axis: Execute returns a stream of ExecuteResponse (a progress|completed oneof) — interim ExecuteProgress liveness updates then a terminal ExecuteCompleted. The references run a tiny work_spec (JSON {steps, rows, indeterminate, fail}): they emit `steps` progress messages (fraction (i+1)/steps, or ABSENT when indeterminate — exercising the proto3 optional double presence) then a terminal completion carrying success + WriteResult.rows_affected, or success=false+error when `fail` is set. The point under test is the STREAMING wire contract (progress framing, optional-fraction presence, terminal oneof), not real compute. NOTE: this axis's references are driven DIRECTLY (not through the stub core gateway). The streaming gap this axis originally surfaced is now CLOSED — ADR-008 added CapabilityInvokeService.InvokeServerStream, which DOES mediate a server-streaming capability; the state axis's stub gateway exercises that relay (plugins/state/inmemory-go/gateway_test.go). The runtime harness stays direct because its job is the streaming WIRE contract (progress framing, optional-fraction presence, terminal oneof), which the relay is transparent to.
